steps.dialogue: Welcome back! Ready to learn more about fractions?
Last time, we introduced two different ways to compare fractions. You learned that when denominators match, you count the pieces. And when numerators match, you compare the piece sizes.

Those two strategies can help us when either the numerator or denominator matches, but what happens when NOTHING matches? No same denominator, no same numerator? That's the question we'll set out to answer today.
When you compare fractions, you have to choose the right strategy for the situation. That thinking will help you tackle even trickier comparisons.
